title: Chestnut cake
subtitle: A dense, earthy Italian-style chestnut flour cake
excerpt: Chestnut cake is a flat, rustic Italian cake made from chestnut flour, olive oil, and water, topped with rosemary and fennel seeds. The batter is thin and bakes into a firm, dense disc with a deep brown crust and an earthy, faintly sweet flavour. It is neither a dessert cake nor a bread but something in between.
sourceNotes: Castagnaccio is a traditional Tuscan and Ligurian cake made from chestnut flour. Documented in nineteenth-century Italian domestic cookery as a peasant staple, chestnut flour being a significant source of carbohydrate in mountain regions before wheat became affordable. The form here follows the classic Tuscan recipe: chestnut flour, water, olive oil, rosemary, and pine nuts.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best on the day. Keeps wrapped for 2 days; the crust softens.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the thin batter bakes quickly

content.text: Chestnut cake (castagnaccio) is made from chestnut flour, water, and olive oil; nothing more than that, plus whatever aromatics go on top. The batter is thin and pours into the tin like pancake batter. It bakes into a firm, slightly chewy disc with a cracked surface and the distinctive earthy sweetness of chestnut flour. Rosemary and fennel seeds on top are traditional; raisins and pine nuts can be pressed in before baking.

items.prepNote: soaked in warm water for 10 minutes, drained

content.text: Whisk the chestnut flour and salt together in a bowl. Add the water in a slow stream, whisking constantly to prevent lumps, then whisk in the olive oil. The batter should be smooth and pourable, roughly the consistency of single cream. Leave to rest for 10 minutes.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Oil a 23 cm cake tin generously and pour in the batter; it should be about 1 cm deep. Scatter the rosemary leaves, fennel seeds, pine nuts, and soaked raisins over the surface. Drizzle a little extra olive oil on top. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes. The cake is done when the surface is cracked in a web pattern, deep brown at the edges, and the centre no longer wobbles. Cool in the tin and serve at room temperature.
